motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Clear and cold, the first real cold of the season. Frost on the dock planking by six, gone by nine. The sky the particular blue that arrives only after the last warmth has been spent, honest and unsentimental.

undercurrent.note: The lake phenomenon has receded into ambient texture. The new strangeness has arrived without announcement: a sealed letter in the Gazette's lost-and-found, addressed to someone who does not yet live here, bearing the Gazette's own address as the return. Wren has noticed it and set it aside. Nobody else knows yet. The undercurrent is very low — this is a thread, not a pull. The town is genuinely calm and ordinary. The geese are back. The frost was on time. The door is open just a crack, and something has slipped a letter through it.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
